etymology.description: 門(門) (mén, "door") + 卝(卝) ("locked door; two wooden sticks to lock a door") -> 門 locked with wooden bars -> semantic 門 (mén, "door") + phonetic 𢇅 (rings on wooden sticks)
etymology.interpretation: Original form depicted a door (門) secured by placing two sticks (卝) across it, representing the concept of closure or fastening. The character evolved, incorporating a phonetic element with additional rings (𢇅) suggesting reinforcement and structure, emphasizing the act of closing or securing.

parts.english: locked door; two wooden sticks to lock a door

examples.chinese: 请把门关上(請把門關上)。
她总是小心地关窗(她總是小心地關窗)，以免生病(以免生病)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zǒng shì xiǎoxīn de guān chuāng, yǐmiǎn shēngbìng.
examples.english: She always carefully closes the window to avoid getting sick.

examples.chinese: 电视机关掉了(電視機關掉了)，家里突然安静了下来(家裡突然安靜了下來)。
examples.pinyin: Diànshì jī guān diào le, jiālǐ tūrán ānjìng le xiàlái.
examples.english: The TV was turned off, and the house suddenly became quiet.
